details.description: A large public parade passes through a neoclassical square in central Brussels. Crowds line metal barriers while police supervise. Several Belgian Armed Forces armored vehicles drive along tram tracks beneath overhead wires: a camouflaged 4x4 scout/command vehicle in front (covered with camouflage netting) and larger wheeled 8x8 armored vehicles with turrets behind. Belgian and EU flags decorate the street. The backdrop is a pale cream neoclassical building with a triangular pediment containing a roundel, slate roof with dormers, and wrought‑iron balcony rails—part of the uniform 18th‑century ensemble around the square. Ornate cast‑iron streetlamps and an overcast sky complete the scene.

details.description: The photo captures a crowded central Brussels street during a ceremonial parade. A line of armored military vehicles, camouflaged with netting, moves along tram tracks while uniformed personnel and a dense onlookers crowd the sidewalks on both sides. Flags, including the Belgian national banner and the European flag, flap from a cream-colored neoclassical building with balconies on the left. Overhead, tram wires cross the street and ornate lampposts line the sidewalk. The overcast sky and the densely packed spectators give a sense of a formal public event in a historic urban setting, likely in the Mont des Arts / Place Royale area of Brussels.

details.description: A street-level photograph capturing a military parade on a cloudy day in Brussels. In the foreground, a large crowd of spectators stands behind metal barriers on the left side of the frame, many holding up smartphones to capture the scene. A police officer with arms crossed stands near the crowd. The center of the image is dominated by a column of military vehicles moving along tram tracks on a paved street. The lead vehicle is an armored personnel carrier heavily draped in camouflage netting, obscuring its specific details. Behind it, several 8x8 armored vehicles (likely MOWAG Piranha IIIC) are visible. In the background, a grand cream-colored neoclassical building, the Hôtel Errera, stands prominently. It features a triangular pediment, a slate roof, and a balcony adorned with flags: the yellow and black Lion of Flanders flag, the Belgian tricolor, and the European Union flag. Stone lion statues sit atop the gate pillars in front of the building. Vintage-style black street lamps line the street.

details.description: A military parade is taking place on a city street lined with neoclassical buildings under an overcast sky. In the foreground, a dense crowd of spectators watches as camouflaged military vehicles, including a Dingo 2 armored car and Piranha IIIC infantry fighting vehicles, drive along tram tracks. A Belgian police officer in a blue uniform stands guard. The prominent building on the left features a cream-colored facade, a pediment with a circular window, and balconies adorned with lion sculptures and various flags, including the Belgian national flag and the European Union flag. The architecture is consistent with the late 18th-century urban planning of central Brussels.

details.description: A crowded city street scene during a military parade in central Brussels. Large groups of spectators stand behind metal barriers while police officers supervise. Several Belgian Army armored vehicles—one light reconnaissance vehicle covered in camouflage netting and two larger 8x8 armored carriers—drive along tram tracks under overhead wires. The backdrop is a cream-colored neoclassical building with a triangular pediment (with round oculus), mansard roof and wrought-iron balconies. Belgian tricolor flags hang from the facade. The atmosphere is overcast, suggesting a public celebration near the Royal Palace/Place des Palais area.

details.description: A large crowd lines a major ceremonial street for a military parade. Two camouflaged armored vehicles move along tram tracks under overhead wires. The setting is an urban square/street with ornate historic lampposts and uniform neoclassical façades: light-colored stucco, mansard roofs with dormers, and a central pedimented building displaying multiple flags (Belgium and regional/EU flags). The view looks along the street toward distant modern office towers. Weather is overcast.

details.description: A vertical shot (rotated 90 degrees counter-clockwise) of a street scene during the Belgian National Day parade in Brussels. A dense crowd of spectators lines the foreground (right side of image), standing behind barriers and watching the procession. On the street, military trucks covered in camouflage netting are driving past. In the background (left side of image), a large neoclassical building with a pale yellow facade, slate roof, and dormer windows is visible. A central balcony on the building displays Belgian and European Union flags. Tram tracks and overhead lines run along the cobbled street. The sky is overcast.

details.description: A high-angle, slightly tilted shot of a military parade in Brussels, Belgium. The scene is captured from the perspective of a spectator in a dense crowd lining the street. Several military armored vehicles, heavily draped in green camouflage netting, are driving along the road, which features tram tracks. In the background, the Neoclassical facade of the Palace of the Nation (Palais de la Nation) is visible, adorned with multiple Belgian and European Union flags. The sky is overcast and grey. People in the crowd are watching the procession, some taking photos with their phones. A police officer in a white and blue uniform stands near one of the vehicles.

details.description: A crowded city street scene during a military parade. Camouflage-clad armored vehicles roll past behind metal barriers while large crowds watch and take photos. Elegant neoclassical buildings with mansard roofs line the street; one corner building displays multiple flags including several Belgian tricolors (black–yellow–red) and an EU flag. Ornate historic lampposts and overhead tram wires are visible, indicating a central European urban square. The sky is overcast.
